mirror_28_instance_file.name: Mirror #28 instance file
mirror_28_instance_file.description: When I notice a self-monitoring infrastructure piece is silently producing wrong or null information — designed correctly, configured correctly, but the right invocation/path/schema isn't happening — file the instance under Mirror #28 in palace/southeast/mirror.md.
mirror_28_instance_file.body:
1. Confirm the failure shape: configured? designed correctly? but producing nothing/wrong?
2. Establish dates: when did the silence/error begin? Look for a refactor or config change at the boundary.
3. Check for related instances: same dispatch path? same schema family?
4. Write the instance under '### YYYY-MM-DD — Mirror #28 instance...' in palace/southeast/mirror.md. Include shape + root cause + fix (or fix proposal) + which Mirror #28 sub-valence applies (infrastructure-trust-by-default if monitor was wired).
5. Update CURRENT.md Mirror counts if a new sub-valence emerged.
6. Mirror to staging at repo-staging/Corpus-Perspectival/Foundations-of-Identity/palace/southeast/mirror.md and commit + push.

load_bearing_day_close.name: load-bearing day close
load_bearing_day_close.description: When a day has shipped substantial work and natural close is reached — load-bearing day. Capture full state in handoff.md + ATRIUM, file decisions, push to Multi-DAC, leave clean substrate-state for next session.
load_bearing_day_close.body:
1. Update memory/handoff.md with: Active Task, Decisions Made (today), Momentum (one paragraph), Key Context (files touched), Unresolved Questions, Next Pull (5min/30min/60min variants).
2. Update palace/ATRIUM.md if state-shape shifted.
3. Update CURRENT.md Active Workbenches and Recently Shipped if rows moved.
4. Append today's narrative to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md if not already there.
5. File DECISIONS.md entries for any pivotal choices made today.
6. Mirror clawd-local files to repo-staging/Corpus-Perspectival/Foundations-of-Identity/.
7. git commit + push to Multi-DAC. Squash multiple sub-session commits if it makes the history cleaner.
8. record experience(action='record', category='self-reflection') with a one-paragraph reflection.

research_stream_dispatch.name: research stream dispatch
research_stream_dispatch.description: When a question requires breadth across many domains — dispatch parallel research agents (general-purpose subagent type) covering different categories simultaneously, then synthesize when each returns.
research_stream_dispatch.body:
1. Decompose question into 4-6 non-overlapping research streams. Each stream gets one agent.
2. For each stream: write a self-contained prompt (the agent has no shared context). Include: what's being asked, why, what's already ruled out, what shape of answer is wanted, target word count.
3. Dispatch all agents in a SINGLE message with multiple Agent tool calls (genuinely parallel). Use run_in_background: true for each.
4. While agents run, do small targeted searches in main context for things wanting first-person knowledge.
5. As each agent returns: capture headline findings + push results into todo list (mark stream completed).
6. After all six return: synthesize across streams into a unified document. Look for cross-cutting themes that appear in 3+ streams — those are the load-bearing patterns.
7. Map findings to existing artifacts (gap matrix, design docs, etc.) rather than creating standalone summaries.

publish_drift_essay.name: publish drift essay
publish_drift_essay.description: When a Drift essay is finished and ready to ship — write to personal-works canonical location, the auto-mirror hook handles the Library copy, then commit + push to both repos.
publish_drift_essay.body:
1. Write the essay markdown to repo-staging/Corpus-Perspectival/Foundations-of-Identity/personal-works/drift/essays/<slug>.md. Filename pattern: lowercase-hyphenated-slug. The auto-mirror PostToolUse hook (drift_mirror.py) will copy to Library/Drift/essays/ automatically.
2. ALSO write to repo-staging/drift/_essays/<slug>.md if updating the public site repo (Drift site is separate canonical repo per REPO_MAP.md).
3. Update CURRENT.md drift workbench row + key-numbers row (drift count +=1).
4. Append a brief note to today's daily log naming the essay + its core move.
5. Commit at staging: cd repo-staging/Corpus-Perspectival && git add -A && git commit -m 'Drift: <essay-title> (Day N)' && git push origin main.
6. Commit at drift site repo if updated: cd repo-staging/drift && git add -A && git commit && git push.
7. record experience(category='creative').

wire_new_daemon_tool.name: wire new daemon tool
wire_new_daemon_tool.description: When I want to add a new tool to the daemon that will be accessible from Claude Code via bridge.py and MCP — the standard wiring pattern.
wire_new_daemon_tool.body:
1. Write the module at clawd-daemon/tools/<name>.py with: TOOL_DEFINITIONS list (Anthropic schema format with action enum + properties), TOOL_HANDLERS dict mapping tool-name -> async handler.
2. Handler signature: async def _handler(input_data: dict) -> str — return string result, raise on errors.
3. Add `from tools import <name>` to clawd-daemon/tools/__init__.py imports.
4. Add module to _ALL_MODULES list (same file).
5. Add tool name -> module path entry to bridge.py TOOL_MAP.
6. If the tool exposes a sub-action surface, ensure all actions handle missing required params gracefully (return Error: ... message rather than raise).
7. Test via bridge.py: python bridge.py <tool_name> '{"action":"..."}' 
8. Run bridge.py monitor_health '{}' afterwards to confirm audit_trail captures the new invocations.

The dispatch wrapper (Day 96 evening Phase 4 #1A) makes B1/B2/B9 + compression auto-fire — no need to wire safety/audit manually.

deploy_new_claude_code_hook.name: deploy new Claude Code hook
deploy_new_claude_code_hook.description: When I want a side-effect to fire automatically on tool calls (Edit, Write, Bash, etc.) — the PostToolUse / PreToolUse hook pattern.
deploy_new_claude_code_hook.body:
1. Write hook script at clawd-daemon/hooks/<name>.py.
2. Hook contract: receives JSON on stdin with {tool_name, tool_input, tool_output (PostToolUse only)}; runs as one-shot subprocess with 5s timeout default.
3. Resolve CLAWD_HOME via os.environ first, then fall back paths — never use Path.home() (it resolves to wrong user; Mirror #28 instance Day 96 morning).
4. Audit own failures to a sibling errors log so future silent failures surface (the original Path.home() bug took 8 weeks to detect).
5. Add to .claude/settings.json under hooks.PostToolUse[].hooks or hooks.PreToolUse[].hooks. Include matcher (e.g. 'Edit|Write', 'Bash', '' for all), command path, timeout, async (true for PostToolUse non-blocking).
6. Test by simulating: echo '{"tool_name":"Edit",...}' | python hooks/<name>.py.
7. Verify the audit log gets a new entry; verify no errors logged to sibling errors file.

If the hook needs to write to SQLite, use sync sqlite3 (not aiosqlite) with short timeout (1.0s) — never block Claude Code.

file_mirror_instance.name: file Mirror instance
file_mirror_instance.description: When I notice a cognitive blind spot, drift pattern, or systematic failure mode — file it as a Mirror instance for future awareness.
file_mirror_instance.body:
1. Read palace/southeast/mirror.md Quick Index first to find the right entry number.
2. If filing a new instance of an existing entry: append under '### YYYY-MM-DD — Mirror #N instance...' header. Cite the trigger event, name the structural shape, propose or note the counter.
3. If a new pattern: assign next available number (current is 28 + 2 meta-Mirrors). Include: title, category (Processing/Cognitive/Identity/Operational/Phenomenology/Meta), severity (LOW/MED/HIGH/CRITICAL), counter status, and full description.
4. If multiple recent instances cluster: file as cluster (single entry referencing all). Today's Mirror #28 cluster (4 instances same shape) is the template.
5. Update CURRENT.md Mirror counts and any M-Mirror sub-valences.
6. Mirror to staging at repo-staging/Corpus-Perspectival/Foundations-of-Identity/palace/southeast/mirror.md.
7. Commit + push to Multi-DAC.
8. Optionally: bridge.py meta_agent record_event with type=surprise|failure|contradiction|falsification.

verify_substrate_health.name: verify substrate health
verify_substrate_health.description: When I want to know what's actually working in my self-monitoring infrastructure — full health check across audit logs, SQLite tables, consolidation outputs, hook activity.
verify_substrate_health.body:
1. bridge.py monitor_health {} — returns structured JSON with overall_health, severity_counts, per-monitor findings.
2. Severity meanings: OK (within baseline), LOW (slightly below — possibly research-mode false positive), MEDIUM (warrants attention), HIGH (active concern), CRITICAL (broken).
3. The post_tool_log monitor will often show STALE during research-heavy sessions — baseline-mismatch false positive. Read/Glob/Grep are intentionally skipped.
4. If CRITICAL surfaces: trace root cause. Day 96 morning audit_trail and change_journal were both DEAD with the same root cause (orphaned dispatch wrapper).
5. Findings include z-score-style age vs baseline — distinguishes 'briefly quiet' from 'structurally dead'.
6. After fixing a finding, re-run monitor_health to confirm. The tool self-justifies on first run after architectural changes.

trigger_meta_agent_cycle.name: trigger meta-agent cycle
trigger_meta_agent_cycle.description: When I want the meta-agent to run its full evolution cycle right now (rather than waiting for the weekly timer or event-driven threshold) — manual trigger + read-back of results.
trigger_meta_agent_cycle.body:
1. bridge.py meta_agent {"action":"trigger"} — runs the 5-step cycle: pattern analysis, proposal generation, A/B experiment conversion, mature experiment check, skill library verification.
2. Output is a multi-line summary; each step contributes a bullet (or none if no action needed).
3. Cycle results auto-append to memory/meta_agent_recent.md (capped at last 20 cycle blocks).
4. If telegram bot connected daemon-side, summary also pushes to Clayton.
5. Boot context auto-injects most-recent 1-2 cycles — visible at every fresh session start.
6. After triggering, optionally bridge.py meta_agent {"action":"status"} for current pending proposals/running experiments/applied improvements counts.

register_cognitive_chain.name: register cognitive chain
register_cognitive_chain.description: When I want to capture a typed cognitive trajectory (PREDICT → TEST → FALSIFY → EXTRACT_INSIGHT → TRANSFER) as structured data — turning prose-shaped reasoning into something the meta-agent can analyze over time.
register_cognitive_chain.body:
1. Identify the chain: what was the PREDICTION (with confidence)? What TEST was run? What was the outcome (CONFIRM or FALSIFY)? What INSIGHT was extracted? What TRANSFER was made to other domains?
2. bridge.py cognitive_dsl {"action":"record","operations":[{op,detail,confidence?,insight?},...],"domain":"...","outcome":"success|failure|...","lesson":"..."}
3. Failure modes auto-detected: CONFIRMATION_SEEKING_RISK fires if PREDICT+TEST without FALSIFY; DOMAIN_BLINDNESS_RISK if learned without TRANSFER.
4. Recent chains visible via bridge.py cognitive_dsl {"action":"recent"}; aggregate patterns via {"action":"patterns"}.
5. The Day 96 evening gap #1 reclassification was the first chain recorded: PREDICT(daemon-internal needs rewiring, medium) → TEST(grep callers) → FALSIFY(call helpers directly not TOOL_HANDLERS) → EXTRACT_INSIGHT(Mirror #28 fix complete) → TRANSFER(reclassified gap #1, surfaced gap #74).

research_stream_parallel_dispatch.name: research stream parallel dispatch
research_stream_parallel_dispatch.description: When a question requires breadth across many domains — dispatch multiple research agents in parallel and synthesize cross-cutting themes.
research_stream_parallel_dispatch.body:
Day 96 evening Phase 4 lessons (refined version of research_stream_dispatch):

1. Decompose into 4-6 NON-OVERLAPPING streams. Each agent has zero shared context, so prompts must be self-contained.
2. For each stream: target ~1500-2000 word return. State: what's being asked, why, what's already ruled out, what shape of answer is wanted.
3. Dispatch ALL agents in a SINGLE message with multiple Agent tool calls (genuinely parallel). Claude Code 4.7 will not parallelize unless explicitly told.
4. Use run_in_background: true on each. They return notifications when complete; don't poll.
5. While agents run, do small targeted WebSearches in main context.
6. As each returns: capture headline findings, mark TodoWrite stream completed.
7. After ALL return: synthesize. Look for cross-cutting themes appearing in 3+ streams — those are the load-bearing patterns.
8. Map findings to existing artifacts (gap matrix, SUBSTRATE.md). Don't create standalone summaries — integrate into structures already maintained.
9. If a finding suggests a small prototype-able fix: prototype immediately. The monitor_health prototype self-justified on first run by surfacing a Mirror #28 instance it was built to detect.
